Sliding-Window CNN for CDI Prediction

Two scripts that train a temporal CNN to classify infants as top or bottom 25% vocabulary scorers at 18 months (CDI), and then visualise which time-series windows the model found most diagnostic.

---

## Scripts

**`train_sliding_window_cv.py`**
Trains the model under leave-one-out cross-validation (LOO-CV). For each held-out participant the model is trained on the remaining participants, then evaluated on the test participant. Selected slices are saved to a `.pt` file for downstream analysis.

**`analyze_slices.py`**
Loads the saved slices from the training script and produces per-channel mean time-series plots and heatmaps, split by class and selection method (prob/attr × top/bottom). Outputs CSV files of mean and standard-error values and PNG plots into a specified directory.

---

## Requirements

```
torch
numpy
pandas
scikit-learn
captum
tqdm
matplotlib
```

---

## Usage

### Step 1: Train and collect slices

```bash
python train_sliding_window_cv.py \
    --data-age 11m \
    --cdi-path CDI_data_w_18_withimputed.csv \
    --time-series-dir time_series_data \
    --window-size 25 \
    --stride 12 \
    --epochs 5 \
    --target-class top
```

Key arguments:

| Argument | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `--data-age` | required | `11m` or `12m` |
| `--cdi-path` | `CDI_data_w_18_withimputed.csv` | CSV with `participant` and `us18_imp` columns |
| `--time-series-dir` | see above | Folder containing `p<id>_<age>.csv` time-series files |
| `--window-size` | 25 | Sliding window length in time steps |
| `--stride` | 12 | Step between consecutive windows |
| `--epochs` | 5 | Training epochs per LOO fold |
| `--target-class` | `top` | `top` = classify top 25% vs rest; `bottom` = bottom 25% vs rest |

Output: `correct_slices_<age>_<target-class>.pt`

### Step 2: Analyse slices

```bash
python analyze_slices.py \
    --slices-file correct_slices_11m_top.pt \
    --channels-file time_series_data/p13_11m.csv \
    --out-dir slice_analysis
```

Key arguments:

| Argument | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `--slices-file` | `correct_slices_11m_top.pt` | Output from Step 1 |
| `--channels-file` | `p13_11m.csv` | Any participant file used to read channel names |
| `--out-dir` | `slice_analysis/` | Directory for output plots and CSVs |

---
